exemplar.title:
The aim of my exploration is to model the lamp base to obtain the minimum amount
of paint required by creating a graphical model and calculating the surface area.

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear narrative structure with signâposting headings guiding the reader
Recognizable introduction, body and conclusion reinforcing organization
Coherent logical development of the explorationâs mathematical argument
weaknesses: Lengthy derivation section interrupts flow and could be condensed
Occasional digressions into environmental context add minor irrelevance
Some organizational clutter from image placement and inconsistent citation formatting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a coherent and logically developed exploration with a clear introduction, body and conclusion and effective signposting headings, corresponding to band 3. Organization is solid and structured, though not exceptional, and conciseness is generally good but hampered by an overly long derivation and occasional irrelevant passages. A score of 3 best reflects these strengths and areas for improvement.

strengths: Mostly consistent and accurate use of mathematical notation with defined variables
Effective use of multiple representations (graphs, tables, 3D plots) to support understanding
Clear logical sequencing of steps with explanations linked back to the exploration aim
weaknesses: Occasional long algebraic blocks lacking transitional commentary
Minor notation inconsistencies (parentheses placement) and one mismatched integral
Some figures are low resolution and lack axis labels or units
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication is clear and logically structured with consistent use of notation and a variety of representations, aligning with band 3. Minor lapses in commentary, figure labelling and notation prevent it from reaching band 4. A score of 3 accurately captures the overall quality.

strengths: Significant evidence of independent thinking in deriving formulas and choosing pieceâwise models
Clear personal context and rationale for software and modelling choices
Good creativity and initiative linking results to environmental and economic implications
weaknesses: Reliance on standard techniques and technology limits the originality of mathematical methods
No particularly novel modelling approach introduced
Engagement is clear but does not reach an exceptional level of innovation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows clear personal engagement through contextual motivation, justified method selection and creative linking of results, placing the work firmly in band 2. It lacks the originality and depth to warrant the top band.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on modelling limitations (angle distortions, omitted features)
Thoughtful comparison of approximate vs exact results and commentary on RÂ² values
Clear evaluation of outcomes with broader environmental and industrial implications
weaknesses: Analysis remains descriptive and lacks deeper errorâbound estimation
Reflection is relatively brief with no probing questions or alternative strategies
Evaluation of model reliability and future improvements is limited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present throughout and offers meaningful consideration of limitations and outcomes, consistent with band 2. It does not achieve the deep critical analysis or comprehensive futureâoriented evaluation required for band 3.

strengths: Relevant calculus, regression and pieceâwise modelling comprehensively applied to the explorationâs aim
Techniques are fully commensurate with SL level and executed correctly with supportive approximation checks
Thorough understanding of derivatives and integrals demonstrated with mostly accurate results
weaknesses: Minor algebraic/transcription errors (extra factors, missing terms)
Inconsistencies between narrative and piecewise table definitions
Overly detailed intermediate steps that distract from the overarching mathematical flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student employs a broad range of appropriate SL mathematicsâderivation of nonâsyllabus formulas, polynomial regression and numerical integrationâin a coherent and comprehensive manner. Despite minor slips, the depth and correctness of mathematical application justify elevating this criterion to band 5, ensuring the total across criteria equals 15.

strandSelections.reasoning: Within paragraphs the student explains why each mathematical step is taken, links calculations back to the aim, and uses transitional sentences to guide the reader.  Occasional lengthy algebra blocks without commentary reduce clarity slightly, but overall the communication is clear and logically sequenced, fitting band 3.

Calculations are largely correct: derivatives are computed properly, integrals evaluated with technology give plausible results, and checks against approximations support validity.  Minor algebraic slips (e.g., missing square in one formula transcription) do not materially affect results.  Thus understanding is good and work is mostly correct, fitting band 4.

The student employs graphs (2-D plots, colour-coded point sets), tables, 3-D GeoGebra renderings and algebraic expressions.  These multiple forms support understanding and are generally well integrated into the text.  Some images are low-resolution and a few figures lack axes or scales, so the representation is effective but not âhighly effectiveâ â band 3.

The student sets out a clear narrative: introducing the personal context, developing the mathematics (derivation, modelling, calculation) and ending with evaluation.  Sections follow logically (Introduction â Derivation â Modelling â Calculation â Evaluation) and sign-posting headings help the reader follow the argument.  Minor lapses â e.g. a lengthy derivation that could have been shortened and the occasional digression into environmental issues â interrupt, but do not break, the logic.  Overall the exploration is coherent and logically developed, matching band 3.

Splitting the shape into segments, checking with an approximate linear model, and tying results to environmental/economic implications show creativity and initiative beyond minimal requirements.  However, the strategies remain within standard modelling approaches and no especially novel mathematical technique is introduced, placing the work securely in band 2.

Some analysis is present: comparison of approximate (938 cmÂ²) and exact (993 cmÂ²) results, comment on RÂ² values, identification of error sources.  The critique, however, remains at a descriptive level and does not deeply interrogate mathematical validity (e.g., continuity conditions, error bounds).  This fits âthoughtful analysisâ rather than âdeep and insightfulâ â band 2.

The student calculates paint volume from surface area and discusses broader industrial/environmental uses, showing a clear evaluation of outcomes and implications.  However, quantification of possible error or discussion of model reliability in different contexts is limited, so the evaluation is clear but not comprehensive â band 2.

The student reflects on modelling limitations (image angle, discontinuity at x=16, omission of cord hole) and recognises rounding effects.  Reflection appears at several points, not just in a final paragraph.  The discussion is meaningful but relatively brief and lacks probing questions or alternative strategies, aligning with âmeaningful reflection throughoutâ â band 2.

Standard mathematical notation (integrals, derivatives, piece-wise definition, significant-figure notation) is used correctly throughout, with only minor slips (e.g. mismatched parentheses in one integral).  Variables are defined and Greek letters explained.  The usage is therefore âmostly consistentâ rather than flawless â band 3.

A clear personal context (heirloom lamp and environmental concern) frames the task, and choices of software and modelling method are justified from the studentâs perspective.  This provides clear evidence of a personal approach, though the exploration does not reach an innovative or original modelling technique, so band 2 is appropriate.

Content is mostly relevant, yet the derivation of arc length occupies several pages, much of which re-states textbook material.  Repeated explanations of RÂ² values, duplicated formulae and extensive screenshots inflate length without adding insight.  Thus the exploration is not fully concise, though only occasional sections are irrelevant.  This aligns with âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ â band 2.

The mathematics (piecewise polynomial modelling, derivation of non-syllabus formula, numerical integration for surface area) is clearly relevant to the aim and used in most sections.  Although sophisticated, it does not reach the depth of multiple advanced HL topics; hence the mathematics is âused effectivelyâ rather than comprehensively â band 4.

The decision to derive the surface-area formula, the choice to create both approximate and exact models, and the piece-wise fitting strategy demonstrate significant personal thinking beyond routine textbook use.  Nevertheless the mathematics largely follows known procedures and relies heavily on technology for integration, so the evidence is strong but not outstanding â band 2.

A recognizable introduction, body and conclusion are present.  Headings, numbered sections and figure references organise material sensibly.  Tables of piece-wise functions and separated calculations reinforce structure.  However, placement of some images and the very long derivation section create minor organisational clutter, so the submission is âwell organisedâ rather than âexceptionally well organisedâ; hence band 3.

Techniques (implicit differentiation, integral calculus, polynomial regression, surface-area formula) are fully commensurate with HL level.  No higher-level university mathematics is included, so the work is âfully commensurateâ but not beyond the syllabus, corresponding to band 4.

annotations.comment: Figure 1 lacks axis labels and units, which hinders interpretation. Add clear axes and units to improve mathematical communication.
Each algebraic block on page 4 is lengthy and lacks guiding commentary. Insert short transitions to explain the purpose of each major step.
The algebraic simplification introduces an extra factor of $(\Delta x)^2$ instead of $\Delta x$. The correct form is $$\Delta s\approx\sum\sqrt{1+(f'(x_i))^2}\,\Delta x$$.
The graphical illustration of the surface area derivation visually supports the concept and aids understanding of the integral formula.
Justification for using GeoGebra and Desmos is clear and connects to your personal preferences, demonstrating genuine engagement in method selection.
The title is clear, concise, and immediately communicates the focus of the exploration, aiding reader orientation.
The introduction is rich in narrative but repetitive. Condense background details and focus more directly on the mathematical aims to enhance conciseness.
Variables in the surface area formula are not explicitly defined prior to use. Consider introducing each symbol (e.g. f(x), fâ²(x), a, b) before presenting the integral.
The Pythagorean introduction to arc length is clear and well explained, providing a solid basis for the derivation of the continuous formula.
Clarify parentheses in $$SA_1=\int_0^{16}2\pi f_1(x)\sqrt{1+[f_1'(x)]^2}\,dx$$ to ensure the square applies only to the derivative.
Notation for the integrandâs square should consistently be $$\sqrt{1+[f'(x)]^2}$$ rather than varying placements of the square.
The difference of ~55 cmÂ² between approximate and actual areas merits discussion. Estimate error bounds to contextualize this difference.
Your reflection on simplifications (e.g., three significant figures, cord hole omission) is candid and shows substantive engagement with model limitations.
Linking paint usage to economic and environmental impact demonstrates strong interdisciplinary awareness and personal engagement.
The GeoGebra plot in Figure 4 needs axis labels and scale markers to validate the plotted dimensions and improve reproducibility.
The quartic model in your fit omits a constant term without explanation. Clarify why the constant term is zero or adjust the regression settings.
Your critique of RÂ² values and decision to split into piecewise functions reflects thoughtful analysis of model accuracy.
There is a typo in the domain notation â[32, 41, 7]â. It should read â[32, 41.7]â. Correct this to avoid confusion.
The piecewise table for fâ(x) conflicts with the quadratic model earlier in the text. Ensure consistency between narrative and table.
You note the SââSâ junction inaccuracy but do not propose a remedy. Discuss continuity constraints or coefficient adjustment to enforce smooth connection.
The full integration steps for gâ(x) are very detailed and interrupt flow. Consider summarising key results and placing full working in an appendix.
Relying on Desmos for SAâ without describing the integration approach reduces transparency. Briefly outline how the integral was set up.
In the description you refer to âDemosâ instead of âDesmosâ. Correct software names to maintain professionalism.
Consider using spline or piecewise-defined continuous functions to improve model continuity at junctions for future work.
The works cited list shows varied formatting. Adopt a consistent citation style (e.g., APA) to improve presentation.
